title: Bumble Q3 2024 Earnings Call Summary
text: Bumble reported Q3 2024 total revenue of $274 million, down 1% year-over-year. The company exceeded adjusted EBITDA expectations at $83 million with a 30% margin, up 290 basis points from the prior year. Key metrics: Total paying users grew 11% to 4.3 million. Bumble app revenue declined 1% to $220 million. Adjusted EBITDA increased 10% to $83 million. CEO Lidiane Jones outlined three core priorities: Ecosystem Strengthening - The company rebalanced marketing toward organic channels in mature markets and tested targeted acquisition in emerging European markets, achieving high single-digit incremental increase in women registration. Customer Experience - Enhanced safety features including improved photo moderation and faster issue routing. The company launched a date on your term product update featuring new matching algorithms and AI-based customer service tools. Revenue Evolution - Testing new monetization approaches to align subscription value with customer success, focusing on conversion improvements rather than aggressive pricing. For full-year 2024, the company projects: Total revenue $1.066-$1.072 billion (1.6% growth). Bumble app revenue $861-$865 million (2% growth). At least 200 basis points adjusted EBITDA margin expansion.
